summary: Cathy O'Neil shares her massive knitting project completed as a coping mechanism for post-election anxiety. She bought yarn the day after the 2016 election and finished the 144-square blanket exactly 100 days after the inauguration. The project served as a way to channel nervous energy and manage overwhelming emotions during a politically turbulent period. She also knitted pussy hats and other projects during the same timeframe.
key_points: Knitting served as a deliberate anxiety management strategy during political upheaval
The project's timeline was symbolically tied to political milestones (election day to 100 days post-inauguration)
The scale of the project (144 squares plus additional items) reflects the intensity of the anxiety being processed
Creative, tactile activities can serve as effective coping mechanisms for political distress
money_quotes: I bought the yarn on the day after the election in an effort to counterbalance my wildly unbalanced thoughts and emotions.
It was a very successful coping mechanism for anxiety.
Because, dude, that's a lot of nervous energy.
Serious question, what do non-knitters do to deal with their anxiety?
key_insight: Creative projects with tangible, repetitive work can serve as powerful coping mechanisms during periods of political anxiety and emotional turmoil.
